name: Talliki Vandanam
description: Financial assistance of ₹15,000/year to mothers whose children attend government schools. TDP Super Six scheme replacing Amma Vodi.
benefits: ₹15,000 per year per child enrolled in a government school (min 75% attendance required)
apply_at: Child's school headmaster — no direct application by parent needed
documents: Aadhaar card of mother (must be linked to bank account)
Child's school enrollment certificate (TC or admission letter)
application_steps: Step 1: Visit your child's school and inform the headmaster you want to register for Talliki Vandanam.
Step 2: Headmaster enters data on the official portal nadunedu.se.ap.gov.in with student Aadhaar.
Step 3: Mandal Education Officer (MEO) verifies the data.
Step 4: Ensure your bank account is Aadhaar-linked and NPCI-enabled before June 12 each year.
Step 5: ₹15,000 is transferred directly to mother's Aadhaar-linked bank account via DBT.
Step 6: If marked ineligible, raise a grievance at your nearest Grama/Ward Sachivalayam or on gsws-nbm.ap.gov.in.
offline_office: Child's school headmaster or nearest Grama Sachivalayam
processing_time: Disbursed annually in June, within 1 week of academic year start
exclusions: Families with government employees, income taxpayers, or four-wheeler owners (except autos) are excluded.

name: Sarvepalli Radhakrishnan Vidyarthi Mitra Kit
description: Free school kit (uniform, shoes, bag, textbooks, notebooks, stationery) for Class 1–10 students in AP government schools. Renamed from Jagananna Vidya Kanuka.
benefits: Free kit worth ₹1,858 per student per year (uniforms, shoes, bag, books, stationery)
apply_at: Automatically distributed at government school — no application needed
documents: School enrollment (distribution is fully automatic — no documents required from student)
application_steps: Step 1: No application required. Kits are automatically distributed at the start of each academic year.
Step 2: Student must be enrolled in an AP Government school (Class 1–10).
Step 3: Kit is distributed on the first day of school by the headmaster.
Step 4: If kit is not received, inform the headmaster or contact the Mandal Education Officer (MEO).
offline_office: School headmaster or Mandal Education Officer (MEO)
processing_time: Distributed at the start of each academic year (June)

name: Post Matric Scholarship – Tuition Fee (RTF)
description: Full reimbursement of tuition fees for SC/ST/BC/EBC/Minority students in higher education. Renamed from Jagananna Vidya Deevena by TDP govt (June 2024).
benefits: Full tuition fee reimbursement for eligible courses at recognised colleges
apply_at: College nodal officer + scholarships.ap.gov.in
application_steps: Step 1: Visit your college's scholarship nodal officer or open scholarships.ap.gov.in.
Step 2: Register with Aadhaar number and mobile number.
Step 3: Fill in the online application form — select RTF (Reimbursement of Tuition Fee).
Step 4: Upload all required documents (caste certificate, income certificate, fee receipt).
Step 5: College principal verifies and forwards application to the Welfare Department.
Step 6: Fee is reimbursed directly to the college account or student bank account.
Step 7: Track status on scholarships.ap.gov.in using your application number.
offline_office: College scholarship nodal officer or District SC/ST/BC Welfare Office
processing_time: 3–6 months after application submission

name: Post Matric Scholarship – Maintenance Fee (MTF)
description: Hostel and food expenses support for SC/ST/BC/EBC/Minority students in higher education. Renamed from Jagananna Vasathi Deevena by TDP govt (June 2024).
benefits: ₹10,000–₹20,000/year for hostel and maintenance charges
apply_at: College nodal officer + scholarships.ap.gov.in
documents: Hostel enrollment proof / hostel fee receipt
application_steps: Step 1: Go to scholarships.ap.gov.in and register with Aadhaar.
Step 2: Select MTF (Maintenance Fee) scheme for your welfare category.
Step 3: Upload hostel enrollment proof and all required documents.
Step 4: College nodal officer verifies your hostel status.
Step 5: Welfare Department disburses amount directly to your bank account.
Step 6: Check disbursement status on scholarships.ap.gov.in.
offline_office: College scholarship nodal officer or District Welfare Office
processing_time: 3–6 months after submission

name: AP Minority Scholarship
description: Central + state scholarship for minority community students in higher education.
benefits: ₹5,000–₹10,000/year scholarship for pre-matric and post-matric courses
apply_at: National Scholarship Portal scholarships.gov.in or AP Minority Welfare Department
application_steps: Step 1: Visit scholarships.gov.in — the National Scholarship Portal (NSP).
Step 2: Click 'New Registration' and register with Aadhaar-linked mobile number.
Step 3: Login and fill the scholarship application form under 'Ministry of Minority Affairs' schemes.
Step 4: Upload religion certificate, income certificate, and admission proof.
Step 5: Submit and note your application ID.
Step 6: Institute verifies the application online.
Step 7: Check payment status on NSP portal using application number.
offline_office: AP Minority Welfare Department District Office or nearest Meeseva Centre
processing_time: 3–4 months from submission deadline

name: Annadata Sukhibhava
description: TDP Super Six flagship scheme providing ₹20,000/year investment support to farmers. Higher than the previous Rythu Bharosa (₹13,500/year).
benefits: ₹20,000 per year investment support in installments. Includes seeds, fertilizers and natural disaster compensation.
apply_at: Grama Sachivalayam / Mandal Agriculture Officer (MAO)
documents: Tenant farming agreement (if applicable for tenant farmers)
application_steps: Step 1: Visit your nearest Grama Sachivalayam with all documents.
Step 2: Request the Digital Assistant (DA) to register you for Annadata Sukhibhava.
Step 3: DA enters your Aadhaar, land records, and bank details into the system.
Step 4: Mandal Agriculture Officer (MAO) verifies land records.
Step 5: Note your Service Request Number for tracking.
Step 6: Check payment status at apagrisnet.gov.in or sspensions.ap.gov.in using Aadhaar.
Step 7: ₹20,000 is released in installments directly to your Aadhaar-linked bank account.
offline_office: Mandal Agriculture Officer (MAO) or Grama Sachivalayam
processing_time: 30–45 days for initial registration; disbursals are seasonal (Kharif/Rabi)

name: PM Kisan Samman Nidhi
description: Central government income support of ₹6,000/year for all landholder farmers in India.
benefits: ₹6,000 per year in 3 equal installments of ₹2,000 directly to bank account
apply_at: pmkisan.gov.in or Grama Sachivalayam or CSC (Common Service Centre)
application_steps: Step 1: Visit pmkisan.gov.in and click 'Farmers Corner' → 'New Farmer Registration'.
Step 2: Enter Aadhaar number, select Andhra Pradesh, and fill in registration form.
Step 3: Upload land records and bank details.
Step 4: Submit — you will get a registration number.
Step 5: Alternatively, visit your nearest Grama Sachivalayam or CSC centre for offline registration.
Step 6: Check payment / beneficiary status at pmkisan.gov.in → 'Beneficiary Status' using Aadhaar or mobile number.
offline_office: Grama Sachivalayam or CSC (Common Service Centre) at Panchayat level
processing_time: 4–8 weeks for first installment after verification

name: AP Free Crop Insurance (PMFBY)
description: AP government pays the farmer's share of the crop insurance premium under PMFBY, making it effectively free for enrolled farmers.
benefits: Premium-free crop insurance. Compensation paid for crop loss due to natural disasters, pests or drought.
apply_at: Nearest bank or Grama Sachivalayam before crop sowing season deadline
documents: Sowing declaration (khasra form filled by village patwari/VRO)
application_steps: Step 1: Apply before the deadline — Kharif deadline is July 31, Rabi deadline is December 31.
Step 2: Visit your nearest bank branch (where loan/KCC is held) or Grama Sachivalayam.
Step 3: Submit Aadhaar, land records, and sowing declaration.
Step 4: Bank or DA registers you on the PMFBY portal — AP government pays your premium share.
Step 5: Collect your policy/enrollment number.
Step 6: In case of crop loss, report within 72 hours by calling 1800-200-7710 or through Grama Sachivalayam.
Step 7: Check claim status on pmfby.gov.in using application ID or Aadhaar.
offline_office: Bank (where KCC/crop loan account is held) or Grama Sachivalayam or Agriculture Department
processing_time: Claims settled within 60 days of crop loss verification

name: AP Free Power Supply – Farmers
description: 9 hours of free daytime electricity for agricultural connections. Budget of ₹4,525 crore allocated. Active under TDP govt.
benefits: 9 hours of free daytime electricity per day for agricultural use (up to 5 HP motor connection)
apply_at: APEPDCL / APSPDCL local section office or Meeseva Centre
application_steps: Step 1: Ensure you have an existing agricultural electricity connection (IP set connection).
Step 2: If not registered, visit your nearest APEPDCL/APSPDCL section office.
Step 3: Submit Aadhaar and land records for Aadhaar-seeding of your electricity account.
Step 4: Once seeded, free power supply benefit is activated automatically — no further steps.
Step 5: For new agricultural connection, apply at Meeseva or DISCOM office with land records.
Step 6: For complaints, call 1912 (toll-free).
offline_office: APEPDCL Section Office (North AP) or APSPDCL Section Office (South AP) or Meeseva Centre
processing_time: Benefit is automatic once connection is registered with Aadhaar

name: AP Free Power Supply – SC/ST Households
description: Free electricity up to 200 units/month for SC and ST households under Babu Jagajeevan Ram Jeevana Jyothi Scheme.
benefits: Free electricity up to 200 units per month for domestic use
apply_at: APEPDCL / APSPDCL local section office or Meeseva Centre
application_steps: Step 1: Visit your nearest APEPDCL/APSPDCL section office or Meeseva Centre.
Step 2: Submit Aadhaar and SC/ST caste certificate for registration.
Step 3: DA seeds your caste data to your electricity consumer account.
Step 4: Free units up to 200/month are automatically applied to your electricity bill.
Step 5: For issues, call 1912 or visit the section office.
offline_office: APEPDCL or APSPDCL Section Office / Meeseva Centre
processing_time: Benefit activated within 15–30 days of registration

name: Aadabidda Nidhi
description: ₹1,500/month direct cash transfer to women from economically weaker sections. TDP Super Six scheme, actively disbursed.
benefits: ₹1,500 per month (₹18,000/year) directly to bank account
apply_at: Nearest Grama Sachivalayam or Ward Secretariat
documents: Aadhaar card (must be linked to bank account)
application_steps: Step 1: Visit your nearest Grama Sachivalayam (rural) or Ward Secretariat (urban).
Step 2: Meet the Digital Assistant (DA) and request registration for Aadabidda Nidhi.
Step 3: Submit Aadhaar, income certificate, ration card, and bank passbook.
Step 4: DA registers you on the GSWS-NBM portal (gsws-nbm.ap.gov.in).
Step 5: Application is verified by the ward/village welfare officer.
Step 6: ₹1,500/month is credited directly to your Aadhaar-linked bank account.
Step 7: Check status at gsws-nbm.ap.gov.in using your Aadhaar number.
offline_office: Grama Sachivalayam or Ward Secretariat (Women & Child Welfare section)
processing_time: 30–45 days from application to first credit

name: Deepam 2.0 – Free LPG Cylinders
description: 3 free LPG cylinders per year reimbursed to eligible women households. TDP Super Six scheme, covering 90 lakh households.
benefits: Cost of 3 LPG cylinders/year reimbursed to bank account within 48 hours of booking
apply_at: Grama Sachivalayam or nearest LPG distributor
documents: LPG connection number (consumer number from gas agency passbook)
application_steps: Step 1: Visit Grama Sachivalayam and register for Deepam 2.0 with your LPG consumer number.
Step 2: DA seeds your Aadhaar with your LPG connection on the portal.
Step 3: When you book a cylinder through your gas agency (phone/app/online), the free benefit is triggered.
Step 4: After delivery, the cylinder cost is reimbursed to your Aadhaar-linked bank account within 48 hours.
Step 5: Free benefit applies to 3 cylinders per year per household.
Step 6: Check reimbursement status at gsws-nbm.ap.gov.in using Aadhaar.
offline_office: Grama Sachivalayam or LPG gas distributor / agency
processing_time: Reimbursement within 48 hours of cylinder delivery

name: APSRTC Free Bus Travel for Women
description: Free travel for all women in APSRTC ordinary and Palle Velugu buses statewide. TDP Super Six scheme.
benefits: Zero-ticket free travel on APSRTC ordinary and Palle Velugu buses anywhere in AP
apply_at: Board the bus directly — no registration required
documents: Any valid photo ID with Andhra Pradesh address (Aadhaar, voter ID, etc.)
Note: No ticket or registration needed — just board and show ID if asked
application_steps: Step 1: No application needed. This is a universal benefit for all women in AP.
Step 2: Board any APSRTC ordinary bus or Palle Velugu bus.
Step 3: Show any AP address proof ID if the conductor asks — Aadhaar or voter ID is sufficient.
Step 4: Free travel is NOT available on AC/Volvo/Express buses.
Step 5: For queries or complaints, call APSRTC helpline: 0866-2570005.
offline_office: Nearest APSRTC Bus Station
processing_time: Immediate — no waiting period

name: NTR Baby Kit
description: Free 11-item baby care kit (worth ₹1,410) given to mothers at discharge from any AP government hospital. Launched May 2025 with G.O.MS.No.61.
benefits: Free baby kit worth ₹1,410 containing 11 essential newborn care items distributed at hospital discharge
apply_portal: N/A — distributed automatically at government hospital
apply_at: Any AP government hospital — automatically given at discharge after delivery
application_steps: Step 1: Deliver at any AP government hospital (PHC, CHC, District Hospital, or Teaching Hospital).
Step 2: Kit is automatically given at the time of discharge — no separate application needed.
Step 3: Kit includes items like soap, towel, nappy, oil, mosquito net, cotton, etc.
Step 4: If kit is not given at discharge, complain to hospital superintendent or call 104.
offline_office: Government hospital nursing staff or hospital superintendent
processing_time: Immediate at discharge

name: Chandranna Pelli Kanuka
description: Financial assistance for marriages of daughters from BPL SC/ST/BC/Minority families. Renamed from YSR Kalyanamasthu by TDP govt (June 2024).
benefits: ₹40,000 to ₹1,00,000 depending on community category and inter-caste combinations
apply_at: Grama Sachivalayam or District SC/ST/BC/Minority Welfare Office — register within 60 days of marriage
documents: Aadhaar cards of bride and groom
10th class certificates of bride and groom (age proof)
application_steps: Step 1: Apply within 60 days of marriage — delays may cause rejection.
Step 2: Visit Grama Sachivalayam with all documents.
Step 3: DA registers application on gsws-nbm.ap.gov.in.
Step 4: District Welfare Officer verifies caste, income, and marriage certificate.
Step 5: Assistance amount is transferred to bride's Aadhaar-linked bank account.
Step 6: Track status at gsws-nbm.ap.gov.in using Aadhaar.
offline_office: District SC/ST/BC/Minority Welfare Office or Grama Sachivalayam
processing_time: 45–90 days after verification

name: AP Aarogyasri Health Scheme
description: Free cashless treatment up to ₹5 lakh/year for BPL families at empanelled hospitals for 2,500+ major procedures and surgeries.
benefits: Free treatment up to ₹5 lakh/year covering 2,500+ procedures at empanelled hospitals
apply_at: Grama Sachivalayam for health card, or directly at any empanelled hospital
application_steps: Step 1: Get your Aarogyasri health card from the Grama Sachivalayam using Aadhaar and white ration card.
Step 2: When treatment is needed, go to any empanelled government or private hospital.
Step 3: Show Aadhaar and Aarogyasri health card at the hospital's Aarogyasri help desk.
Step 4: Hospital pre-authorises the treatment on the Aarogyasri portal.
Step 5: Treatment is provided cashless — up to ₹5 lakh per year per family.
Step 6: For list of empanelled hospitals, visit aarogyasri.ap.gov.in.
Step 7: For complaints or claim status, call 104 (toll-free).
offline_office: Aarogyasri help desk at any empanelled hospital, or Grama Sachivalayam
processing_time: Treatment begins same day at empanelled hospital after pre-authorisation

name: PMJAY – Ayushman Bharat
description: Central government health insurance scheme providing ₹5 lakh/year cashless treatment for BPL and low-income families at empanelled hospitals.
benefits: ₹5 lakh/year cashless health coverage per family for hospitalisation at empanelled hospitals
apply_at: Check eligibility at pmjay.gov.in — apply via CSC or empanelled hospital
documents: Ration card or SECC data inclusion proof
application_steps: Step 1: Check eligibility at pmjay.gov.in → 'Am I Eligible?' using mobile or ration card number.
Step 2: If eligible, get your Ayushman card from nearest CSC (Common Service Centre) or hospital.
Step 3: At the time of hospitalisation, show Aadhaar and Ayushman card at the hospital help desk.
Step 4: Hospital verifies on PMJAY portal and provides cashless treatment.
Step 5: For card download or complaints, call 14555 or visit beneficiary.nha.gov.in.
offline_office: CSC Centre at Panchayat level or empanelled hospital help desk
processing_time: Card issued within 1–2 days; treatment same day at empanelled hospital

name: Janani Suraksha Yojana (JSY)
description: Central NHM scheme providing cash incentive to BPL pregnant women who deliver in government or accredited hospitals.
benefits: ₹1,400 cash incentive for rural institutional delivery + ₹1,000 for urban. ASHA worker support included.
apply_at: Contact nearest ASHA/ANM worker or Government PHC before delivery
documents: Pregnancy registration card (ANC card / Mother & Child protection card)
application_steps: Step 1: As soon as pregnancy is confirmed, register with your local ASHA or ANM (auxiliary nurse midwife) worker.
Step 2: Get your ANC (Ante-Natal Care) card from the nearest PHC or government hospital.
Step 3: Attend at least 3 ANC check-ups at a government facility.
Step 4: Deliver at a government hospital or accredited private hospital.
Step 5: ASHA worker submits disbursement request after delivery.
Step 6: ₹1,400 (rural) or ₹1,000 (urban) is credited to your Aadhaar-linked bank account within 7 days.
offline_office: Local ASHA worker, ANM, PHC, or Government Hospital (maternity ward)
processing_time: Cash incentive credited within 7–15 days of delivery

name: AP 108 Emergency Ambulance Service
description: Free emergency ambulance service (108) available 24x7 across AP for medical emergencies, accidents, and maternity cases.
benefits: Free emergency ambulance transport to nearest government hospital — call 108
apply_at: Dial 108 — no prior registration needed
documents: No documents required — call 108 directly in emergency
application_steps: Step 1: In any medical emergency, accident, or maternity case — dial 108 immediately.
Step 2: Provide your location (village/mandal/nearest landmark) to the dispatcher.
Step 3: Ambulance arrives within 15–20 minutes in urban areas, 30–45 minutes in rural areas.
Step 4: Service is free — no payment required.
Step 5: For non-emergency health queries, dial 104.
offline_office: N/A — call 108 directly
processing_time: Ambulance dispatched immediately after call

name: ESI (Employees State Insurance) Scheme
description: Central social insurance scheme providing medical, maternity, disability and dependent benefits to workers in organised sector establishments.
benefits: Free medical care for employee and family, maternity benefit, sickness benefit (70% wages), disability pension
apply_at: Your employer registers you on esic.gov.in — employee cannot apply directly
application_steps: Step 1: Your employer (if establishment has 10+ employees) must register on esic.gov.in.
Step 2: Employer registers each employee on the ESIC portal — employee gets ESIC Pehchaan card.
Step 3: Use ESIC Pehchaan card at any ESIC dispensary or hospital for free treatment.
Step 4: For maternity benefit, apply at your nearest ESIC branch office with medical certificates.
Step 5: For sickness benefit, get a sickness certificate from ESIC-approved doctor.
Step 6: For queries or missing card, call 1800-11-2526 or visit esic.gov.in.
offline_office: ESIC Branch Office (Vijayawada, Visakhapatnam, Guntur, Tirupati) or ESIC Regional Office AP
processing_time: ESIC card issued within 2–4 weeks of employer registration

name: NTR Bharosa Pension – Old Age
description: Monthly pension of ₹4,000 for senior citizens aged 60+. Increased from ₹3,000 in July 2024 by TDP govt.
benefits: ₹4,000 per month directly to bank account
apply_at: Grama Sachivalayam or Ward Secretariat or sspensions.ap.gov.in
documents: Age proof (birth certificate / school TC / Aadhaar itself)
application_steps: Step 1: Visit your nearest Grama Sachivalayam (village) or Ward Secretariat (town/city).
Step 2: Request the pension application form for 'Old Age Pension' (NTR Bharosa).
Step 3: Submit Aadhaar, age proof, ration card, and bank passbook.
Step 4: DA registers your application on sspensions.ap.gov.in portal.
Step 5: MPDO (rural) or Municipal Commissioner (urban) verifies and sanctions pension.
Step 6: ₹4,000/month is credited directly to your Aadhaar-linked bank account on the 1st of every month.
Step 7: Check pension status at sspensions.ap.gov.in → Search → enter Aadhaar or Pension ID.
offline_office: Grama Sachivalayam, Ward Secretariat, or MPDO Office
processing_time: 30–60 days from application to first disbursement

name: NTR Bharosa Pension – Widow
description: Monthly pension of ₹4,000 for widows aged 18 and above from low-income families.
benefits: ₹4,000 per month directly to bank account
apply_at: Grama Sachivalayam or Ward Secretariat
application_steps: Step 1: Visit Grama Sachivalayam / Ward Secretariat and request Widow Pension application.
Step 2: Submit Aadhaar, husband's death certificate, ration card, and bank passbook.
Step 3: DA registers on sspensions.ap.gov.in and forwards to MPDO.
Step 4: MPDO/Municipal Commissioner verifies and sanctions pension.
Step 5: ₹4,000/month credited directly to bank account.
Step 6: Check status at sspensions.ap.gov.in using Aadhaar or Pension ID.
offline_office: Grama Sachivalayam, Ward Secretariat, or MPDO Office
processing_time: 30–60 days from application

name: NTR Bharosa Pension – Disabled
description: Monthly pension for persons with disabilities. ₹6,000/month for 40%+ disability; ₹15,000/month for 100% disability.
benefits: ₹6,000/month (40%+ disability) or ₹15,000/month (100% total disability)
apply_at: Grama Sachivalayam or Ward Secretariat — SADAREM disability certificate required
documents: SADAREM disability certificate (40%+ disability — from District Hospital)
application_steps: Step 1: If you don't have a SADAREM certificate, visit the District Hospital for a disability assessment.
Step 2: With SADAREM certificate in hand, visit Grama Sachivalayam or Ward Secretariat.
Step 3: DA registers application on sspensions.ap.gov.in with disability type and percentage.
Step 4: MPDO/Municipal Commissioner verifies and sanctions pension.
Step 5: Pension amount is determined by disability percentage — ₹6,000 (40–99%) or ₹15,000 (100%).
Step 6: Check pension status at sspensions.ap.gov.in using Aadhaar or Pension ID.
offline_office: District Hospital (SADAREM) + Grama Sachivalayam or MPDO Office
processing_time: 45–90 days (includes SADAREM certificate process)

name: NTR Bharosa Pension – Single Woman
description: Monthly pension of ₹4,000 for unmarried/separated/deserted women above 18 with no income support.
benefits: ₹4,000 per month directly to bank account
apply_at: Grama Sachivalayam or Ward Secretariat
documents: Declaration/affidavit of single/unmarried/separated status (notarised or village officer certified)
application_steps: Step 1: Visit Grama Sachivalayam / Ward Secretariat.
Step 2: Submit Aadhaar, declaration of single status, ration card, and bank passbook.
Step 3: DA registers application on sspensions.ap.gov.in.
Step 4: MPDO verifies and sanctions pension.
Step 5: ₹4,000/month credited to Aadhaar-linked bank account.
Step 6: Check status at sspensions.ap.gov.in using Aadhaar or Pension ID.
offline_office: Grama Sachivalayam or Ward Secretariat
processing_time: 30–60 days

name: NTR Bharosa Pension – Weavers & Fishermen
description: Monthly pension of ₹4,000 for handloom weavers (age 50+) and fishermen from low-income families.
benefits: ₹4,000 per month directly to bank account
apply_at: Grama Sachivalayam with occupation proof from Handloom or Fisheries Department
documents: Occupation proof — Weaver's identity card (from Handloom Department) OR Fisheries ID card (from Fisheries Department)
application_steps: Step 1: Obtain occupation proof — weavers need a certificate from the Handloom Department; fishermen from the Fisheries Department.
Step 2: Visit Grama Sachivalayam with Aadhaar, occupation proof, ration card, and bank passbook.
Step 3: DA registers application on sspensions.ap.gov.in with occupation category.
Step 4: MPDO verifies occupation certificate and sanctions pension.
Step 5: ₹4,000/month credited to bank account.
Step 6: Check status at sspensions.ap.gov.in using Aadhaar.
offline_office: Grama Sachivalayam + District Handloom/Fisheries Office for occupation certificate
processing_time: 30–60 days after occupation certificate is obtained

name: NTR Housing Scheme
description: Free house construction support for houseless poor families in rural AP. Renamed from YSR Housing (Pedalandru Illu) by TDP govt.
benefits: Free house construction support of ₹1.8–2.5 lakh depending on location and type
apply_at: Grama Sachivalayam or AP Housing Corporation (APHMHIDC) District Office
documents: No house affidavit (notarised declaration that family has no pucca house)
Caste certificate (if SC/ST/BC — for additional priority)
application_steps: Step 1: Visit Grama Sachivalayam and inform DA you want to apply for NTR Housing.
Step 2: Confirm your name is in the beneficiary survey list (from SECC/AP housing survey).
Step 3: If not in list, submit application with all documents — DA adds you to the waiting list.
Step 4: Land/plot allotment is done by the Revenue Department if you don't have land.
Step 5: Construction funds (₹1.8–2.5 lakh) released in 3–4 installments based on construction progress.
Step 6: Track status at housing.ap.gov.in using Aadhaar or application number.
Step 7: Construction must be completed within 24 months of first installment.
offline_office: Grama Sachivalayam, MPDO Office, or APHMHIDC District Office
processing_time: Fund release in installments over 12–24 months

name: PMAY Gramin (Rural Housing)
description: Central scheme providing financial assistance of ₹1.2–1.3 lakh to rural BPL families to build a pucca house.
benefits: ₹1.20–1.30 lakh construction assistance for pucca house in rural area
apply_at: Gram Panchayat or Grama Sachivalayam or pmayg.nic.in
documents: SECC-2011 survey inclusion proof (check at pmayg.nic.in)
application_steps: Step 1: Check if your name is in the SECC-2011 Awaas+ list at pmayg.nic.in → Stakeholders → IAY/PMAYG Beneficiary.
Step 2: If in the list, your Gram Panchayat will call you for registration.
Step 3: If not in list, apply through the Gram Panchayat/Grama Sachivalayam for inclusion.
Step 4: Submit Aadhaar, land proof, and bank details to Gram Panchayat.
Step 5: Funds released in installments to your Aadhaar-linked bank account as construction progresses.
Step 6: Construction completion photo must be uploaded at each stage on AwaasSoft portal.
Step 7: Check payment status at pmayg.nic.in using your Aadhaar or registration number.
offline_office: Gram Panchayat, Grama Sachivalayam, or DRDA District Office
processing_time: Funds released over 12–18 months in installments

name: PMAY Urban (Housing for All)
description: Central scheme providing home loan interest subsidy for urban EWS/LIG/MIG families without a pucca house.
benefits: Interest subsidy up to ₹2.67 lakh on home loan (EWS/LIG). In-situ slum redevelopment also covered.
apply_at: pmaymis.gov.in or your bank branch or ULB (Urban Local Body / Municipal Office)
application_steps: Step 1: Visit pmaymis.gov.in → Citizen Assessment → Apply Online.
Step 2: Enter Aadhaar number and fill the application form.
Step 3: Select your applicable category — EWS (income < ₹3L), LIG (₹3–6L), MIG-I (₹6–12L), MIG-II (₹12–18L).
Step 4: Apply for home loan at a participating bank/NBFC — mention PMAY-CLSS.
Step 5: Bank verifies eligibility and submits subsidy claim to NHB/HUDCO.
Step 6: Interest subsidy (up to ₹2.67 lakh for EWS) is credited upfront to loan account.
Step 7: Track application status at pmaymis.gov.in using Aadhaar or application ID.
offline_office: Urban Local Body (Municipality / Corporation) or Home Loan Bank Branch
processing_time: Subsidy credited within 3–6 months of loan disbursement

name: AP SC/ST Housing Subsidy
description: Additional housing subsidy for SC and ST families for house construction or repair under SCSP/TSP funds of AP government.
benefits: Additional subsidy of ₹50,000–₹1 lakh for house construction or repair under SCSP/TSP allocations
apply_at: District SC/ST Welfare Office or Grama Sachivalayam
application_steps: Step 1: Visit Grama Sachivalayam or District SC/ST Welfare Office.
Step 2: Submit caste certificate, income certificate, land proof, and no-house affidavit.
Step 3: DA or welfare officer registers application on the portal.
Step 4: District SC/ST Welfare Officer verifies and sanctions the subsidy.
Step 5: Subsidy is transferred to your Aadhaar-linked bank account in 1–2 installments.
Step 6: Track at gsws-nbm.ap.gov.in using Aadhaar.
offline_office: District SC/ST Welfare Office (contact District Collector office for address)
processing_time: 45–90 days

name: AP EWS Housing Scheme
description: Housing assistance for Economically Weaker Section families in urban areas through AP state housing board.
benefits: Subsidised/free housing unit allocation to EWS urban beneficiaries via lottery or waiting list
apply_at: AP Housing Board (APHB) District Office or District Collector's Office
application_steps: Step 1: Visit APHB district office or District Collector's office to check current allotment drive.
Step 2: Fill the application form and submit with income certificate and no-house affidavit.
Step 3: Application is reviewed and placed in the waiting list / lottery pool.
Step 4: Allotment is communicated by post or through APHB portal.
Step 5: Subsidised housing unit is handed over after documentation.
Step 6: Check allotment status at aphb.gov.in.
offline_office: AP Housing Board (APHB) District Office or District Collector's office
processing_time: Allotment based on waiting list / lottery — variable

name: AP SC Corporation Loan
description: Low-interest loans for SC community members for business setup or education expenses.
benefits: Loans at 3–4% interest up to ₹1 lakh for business or education
apply_at: AP Scheduled Castes Co-operative Finance Corporation (APCFSS) District Office
application_steps: Step 1: Visit the nearest APCFSS District Office with all documents.
Step 2: Fill the loan application form — specify purpose (business/education).
Step 3: Submit caste certificate, income certificate, and business/education proof.
Step 4: District Officer verifies documents and forwards to sanctioning authority.
Step 5: Loan is credited to your bank account after sanction.
Step 6: For status, call helpline 0863-2347314 or visit apcfss.in.
offline_office: APCFSS District Office — find your district office at apcfss.in
processing_time: 30–60 days after document verification

name: AP ST Corporation Loan
description: Low-interest loans for ST community members for business or agriculture.
benefits: Loans at 3% interest up to ₹1 lakh for business or agriculture
apply_at: AP ST Finance Corporation District Office or ITDA (Integrated Tribal Development Agency) Office
documents: Business plan or agriculture land proof
application_steps: Step 1: Visit the AP ST Finance Corporation district office or ITDA office.
Step 2: Collect and fill the loan application form.
Step 3: Attach ST caste certificate, income certificate, and purpose proof.
Step 4: Application is processed by the district officer.
Step 5: Loan amount is disbursed to your bank account after sanction.
Step 6: Visit aptribes.gov.in or call 0863-2346651 for status.
offline_office: AP ST Finance Corporation District Office or nearest ITDA Office
processing_time: 30–60 days

name: AP BC Corporation Loan
description: Subsidised self-employment loans for BC community members.
benefits: Loans at 4% interest up to ₹1 lakh for self-employment
apply_at: AP BC Welfare Corporation District Office
application_steps: Step 1: Visit the AP BC Welfare Corporation district office.
Step 2: Fill the self-employment loan application form.
Step 3: Submit BC caste certificate, income certificate, and business plan.
Step 4: District Officer verifies documents and forwards for sanction.
Step 5: Loan amount credited to your bank account.
Step 6: Check status at apbcbonalu.ap.gov.in or call 0866-2550055.
offline_office: AP BC Welfare Corporation District Office
processing_time: 30–60 days

name: NTR Nethanna Nestham
description: ₹24,000/year direct financial assistance to handloom weavers owning a loom. Renamed from YSR Nethanna Nestham.
benefits: ₹24,000 per year (₹2,000/month) directly to bank account
apply_at: Grama Sachivalayam or District Handloom & Textile Department Office
documents: Handloom ownership certificate / Weaver identity card (from Handloom Department)
application_steps: Step 1: First obtain a Weaver Identity Card from the District Handloom Department if you don't have one.
Step 2: Visit Grama Sachivalayam with Weaver ID card, Aadhaar, bank passbook.
Step 3: DA registers you on the NTR Nethanna Nestham portal via gsws-nbm.ap.gov.in.
Step 4: Handloom Department verifies loom ownership and weaver status.
Step 5: ₹24,000/year is released in installments directly to your bank account.
Step 6: Check status at gsws-nbm.ap.gov.in using Aadhaar.
offline_office: Grama Sachivalayam or District Handloom & Textiles Department
processing_time: 30–45 days after Weaver ID verification

name: AP Vahana Mitra
description: ₹10,000/year financial assistance to self-employed auto-rickshaw, taxi, and maxi-cab owner-drivers for insurance and maintenance.
benefits: ₹10,000 per year directly to bank account
apply_at: AP Transport Department portal transport.ap.gov.in or nearest RTA (Regional Transport Authority) office
documents: Vehicle Registration Certificate (RC) — vehicle must be in applicant's own name
application_steps: Step 1: Visit transport.ap.gov.in and navigate to Vahana Mitra Scheme.
Step 2: Register with Aadhaar and driving licence number.
Step 3: Upload RC copy, driving licence, and bank details.
Step 4: Application is verified by the RTA of your district.
Step 5: ₹10,000 is credited annually to your Aadhaar-linked bank account.
Step 6: Alternatively, visit your nearest RTO / RTA office for offline registration.
Step 7: Track status at transport.ap.gov.in or call 1800-425-9533.
offline_office: Regional Transport Authority (RTA) Office in your district
processing_time: 30–45 days after RTA verification
